messages.content: 帮我润色论文
当然可以，请提供您的论文内容，我会尽力为您润色和提供改进意见。

As shown in Fig. 1, the proposed system consists of a PV module 
placed on the evaporating section of the HP, while an RC module is 
placed on the condensing section, each module facing the sky. During 
the day, solar radiation reaches the silicon layer (Psun) after transmission 
through the above layers and gets partially converted into electricity 
(Pelec). The remaining solar energy (Psun - Pelec) is the waste heat, and a 
part of it is lost from the top side of the PV module through radiation 
(Prad) and convection (Pconv), while another part of it gets transferred to 
the HP via conduction between the layers. Within the HP, the heat is 
transferred from its evaporating section to the condensing section via a 
phase change process explained later. At the condensing section, the 
heat is released from the top surface of the RC module via radiation to 
the sky (Prad) and convection with the atmosphere (Pconv). 
The idea for this research was inspired by the work of Ezekwe [43], 
who designed a refrigerator using the RC technique integrated with HP. 
However, in that period, RC could only be harnessed during nighttime. 
With the recent emergence of advanced materials and fabrication 
techniques, RC can now be harnessed in the daytime within the presence 
of sunlight since the radiative coolers can simultaneously reflect solar 
radiation and emit thermal radiation. The major novelty for this study is 
the use of such daytime radiative coolers on the condensing section of 
the HP in order to reduce the solar cell operating temperature. In 
conclusion, the efficiency of the PV system is improved as the waste heat 
from the cells is released from both the top of the PV module and the RC 
module, while the HP serves as a heat transfer mechanism between the 
two. The individual module configurations, properties, and working 
principles are explained in the following section.

As shown in Fig. 1, the proposed system is comprised of a PV module situated on the evaporating section of the HP, while an RC module is placed on the condensing section, with both modules facing the sky. During the daytime, solar radiation passes through the various layers and reaches the silicon layer (Psun), where it is partially converted into electricity (Pelec). The remaining solar energy (Psun - Pelec) is considered waste heat, with a portion being lost from the top side of the PV module through radiation (Prad) and convection (Pconv). Another portion of the waste heat is transferred to the HP through conduction between the layers. Within the HP, the heat is transferred from the evaporating section to the condensing section through a phase change process detailed later. At the condensing section, the heat is released from the top surface of the RC module through radiation to the sky (Prad) and convection with the atmosphere (Pconv).

The idea for this research was inspired by Ezekwe's work [43], where a refrigerator was designed using the RC technique integrated with the HP. However, at that time, RC could only be utilized during nighttime. With the recent advancements in materials and fabrication techniques, RC can now be utilized during the daytime in the presence of sunlight, as the radiative coolers are capable of reflecting solar radiation while emitting thermal radiation. The key novelty of this study lies in using these daytime radiative coolers on the condensing section of the HP to reduce the operating temperature of the solar cells. In conclusion, the efficiency of the PV system is improved by releasing waste heat from both the top of the PV module and the RC module, with the HP serving as the mechanism for heat transfer between the two. The individual configurations, properties, and working principles of the modules are explained in detail in the following section.
改写以上论文 尽可能避免相同 但是要保证论文的专业性

As illustrated in Fig. 1, the proposed system comprises of a photovoltaic (PV) module placed on the evaporating section of a heat pipe (HP), while a radiative cooler (RC) module is positioned on the condensing section, both facing the sky. During daylight hours, solar radiation passes through the layers and reaches the silicon layer, where it undergoes partial conversion into electricity. The remaining solar energy is considered as waste heat, with a portion being dissipated from the top side of the PV module through radiation and convection. Meanwhile, another portion is conducted to the HP to facilitate a phase change process for the transfer of heat from the evaporating section to the condensing section. At the condensing section, the heat is released through radiation to the sky and convection with the atmosphere.

The inspiration for this research builds upon Ezekwe's work (43), which introduced a refrigerator incorporating the RC technique within an HP. However, at that time, RC utilization was restricted to nighttime. Recent advancements in advanced materials and fabrication techniques now enable the harnessing of RC during daytime, exploiting the radiative coolers' capability to reflect solar radiation while simultaneously emitting thermal radiation. The main contribution of this study lies in utilizing these daytime radiative coolers on the condensing section of the HP to reduce the operating temperature of the PV cells. Consequently, the overall efficiency of the PV system is enhanced by effectively dissipating waste heat from both the top of the PV module and the RC module. The HP acts as a conduit facilitating the heat transfer between the two modules. Detailed explanations of the individual module configurations, properties, and operating principles are provided in the subsequent section.
翻译成英文，用词需要体现论文的专业性：典型的光伏模块由五层组成: 玻璃、醋酸乙烯基(EVA)、硅太阳能电池、EVA和tedlar-polyester-tedlar(TPT). 玻璃提供机械强度和高透过太阳辐射的能力; EVA是一种胶封材料, 提供不同层之间的粘附性; 太阳能电池通过光伏效应将太阳辐射转化为电能; TPT保护背面.

A typical photovoltaic (PV) module consists of five layers: glass, ethylene-vinyl acetate (EVA), silicon solar cells, EVA, and tedlar-polyester-tedlar (TPT). The glass layer provides mechanical strength and high transmittance of solar radiation. EVA is an encapsulant material that ensures adhesion between different layers. The solar cells convert solar radiation into electrical energy through the photovoltaic effect. TPT serves as a protective backing for the module.

翻译成英文，用词需要体现学术论文的专业性：基于上述假设, 我们为本文所提出的系统构建了数值模型. 
首先, 为PV/T模块构建了如下热力学模型, 一维稳态热扩散方程式如下所示:

Based on the aforementioned assumptions, we constructed a numerical model for the proposed system in this study. First, a thermodynamic model was developed for the PV/T module, employing the following one-dimensional steady-state heat diffusion equation:
继续翻译：其中, T是温度分布, k是热导率, Q是内部热发生率(热源项), 硅电池层中Q为电池工作产生的废热, 即吸收的太阳能量和产生的电能之间的差异, 如下所示:

Where T represents the temperature distribution, k is the thermal conductivity, and Q is the internal heat generation rate (heat source term). Within the silicon cell layer, Q corresponds to the waste heat generated by the cell's operation, which is the difference between the absorbed solar energy and the generated electrical energy, expressed as follows:
继续翻译：照射在光伏模块最顶层的太阳辐射
The solar radiation incident on the top surface of the photovoltaic module.
继续翻译：这是照射在光伏模块最顶层的太阳辐射
This refers to the solar radiation incident on the top surface of the photovoltaic module.
继续翻译：是光伏模块的电转换效率, 根据经验公式有
This is the electrical conversion efficiency of the photovoltaic module, which can be determined by empirical formulas such as:
继续翻译：其中, ηref是参考转换效率(ηref = 0.2); β是晶体硅电池的温度系数(β = 0.0045 K−1); TPV是光伏操作温度, 是硅层的平均温度(K); Tref是参考温度(Tref = 298.15 K). 注意, 随着TPV的升高, ηelec线性下降.

Where ηref is the reference conversion efficiency (ηref = 0.2), β is the temperature coefficient of crystalline silicon cells (β = 0.0045 K−1), TPV is the operating temperature of the photovoltaic module, defined as the average temperature of the silicon layer (K), and Tref is the reference temperature (Tref = 298.15 K). It is important to note that as TPV increases, the electrical efficiency (ηelec) linearly decreases.
